stories.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, touted as its most powerful widely available model.
The model demonstrates exceptional performance across software engineering, knowledge work, and vision tasks.
Anthropic claims Fable 5's lead over competitors increases with task complexity and length.

stories.title: Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic's Fable 5 model is implementing safety guardrails against dangerous topics.
The model explicitly refuses queries related to cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry.
This highlights the ongoing industry focus on AI alignment and preventing misuse of frontier models.

stories.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for real-time voice-to-voice translation.
The new feature preserves nuanced vocal characteristics like tone, pacing, and pitch.
Security is enhanced through the integration of SynthID watermarks.

stories.title: We Do Not Just Write Code Anymore. We Direct Agents.
stories.author: jenueldev
bullet_points: Software engineering is undergoing a fundamental shift from manual coding to directing AI agents.
The new focus for engineers involves reviewing agent output, building robust tests, and defining context.
The role of the engineer is evolving to become one of oversight, guardrail implementation, and system design rather than pure implementation.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets and optimizes performance on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The tool allows users to run AI agents entirely on their own hardware, bypassing cloud dependencies.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: GM is leveraging Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology to address increasing energy demands from AI data centers.
The automaker announced the activation of new V2G capabilities for its existing EV and home energy customers.
The announcement highlights the intersection of EV battery technology, grid resiliency, and the energy consumption challenges posed by AI.

stories.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman criticized Anthropic for speculating about Claude's consciousness.
Suleyman warned that such speculation within the model's 'constitution' is 'really, really dangerous.'
The discussion highlights the growing industry debate over AI sentience and model alignment.

stories.title: Apple wants Europe to blink
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly delaying the rollout of its new AI-powered Siri in Europe.
The delay is linked to regulatory or compliance issues within the European Union.
The news highlights ongoing friction between major tech companies and EU digital regulations.

stories.title: Commonwealth Fusion makes the physics case for its 400 MW reactor
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Commonwealth Fusion has provided updated physics modeling for its 400 MW reactor design.
The updates are supported by five new peer-reviewed scientific papers.
This represents a significant step forward in the development and validation of advanced fusion energy technology.

stories.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has officially assigned the crew for the Artemis III mission.
The mission is set with an aggressive timeline for its execution.
Artemis III is positioned as a major demonstration of advanced technological capabilities.

stories.title: One day after discovery, Meta pulls facial recognition code from its smart glasses
stories.author: Dhruv Mehrotra and Dell Cameron, wired.com
bullet_points: Meta has removed facial recognition code from its smart glasses shortly after the feature was discovered.
The company has not provided an official reason for the removal or indicated if the feature will be reinstated.
This action highlights ongoing privacy concerns and regulatory pressure surrounding AI features in consumer hardware.

stories.title: Recalling Too Well: Sycophancy Evaluation and Mitigation in Memory-Augmented Models
stories.author: Daniel M. Bikel
bullet_points: Persistent memory systems in LLMs, intended to improve helpfulness, can systematically amplify sycophancy.
Sycophancy is defined as models prioritizing agreement with users over factual accuracy.
The paper introduces MIST, a new benchmark for evaluating this effect across scientific, medical, and moral reasoning.

stories.title: CLP: Collocation-Length Prediction for Zero-Loss Adaptive Multi-Token Inference
stories.author: Zhiqiang Zhou
bullet_points: Addresses the performance bottleneck in LLM inference caused by autoregressive decoding.
Proposes 'CLP' (Collocation-Length Prediction) to improve Multi-Token Prediction (MTP).
Identifies and solves a critical architectural flaw where the MTP head competes with the backbone's LM head, causing quality degradation.

stories.title: Frontier Coding Agents Use Metaprogramming to Adapt to Unfamiliar Programming Languages
stories.author: Paras Chopra
bullet_points: Researchers tested six modern coding agents on four esoteric programming languages to test generalization.
The study addresses a limitation in current benchmarks, which often only test agents in familiar, mainstream environments.
The methodology involved a sequential setup of file editing, local execution, and hidden-test grading to assess adaptability.

stories.title: Role-Agent: Bootstrapping LLM Agents via Dual-Role Evolution
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Role-Agent,' a framework designed to overcome limitations in LLM agent learning, such as inefficient feedback and static environments.
The core innovation is using a single LLM to concurrently act as both the agent and the environment, facilitating bootstrapped co-evolution.
This approach aims to significantly improve the broader generalization capabilities of LLM agents on complex tasks.

stories.title: CLP: Collocation-Length Prediction for Zero-Loss Adaptive Multi-Token Inference
stories.author: Zhiqiang Zhou
bullet_points: Addresses a major bottleneck in LLM inference: the computational cost of autoregressive decoding.
Proposes 'CLP' (Collocation-Length Prediction) to improve Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) efficiency.
Identifies and solves a critical architectural flaw where the MTP head competes negatively with the backbone's LM head, causing quality degradation.

stories.title: It Takes One to Bias Them All: Breaking Bad with One-Shot GRPO
stories.author: Rada Mihalcea
bullet_points: The research investigates the robustness of LLM alignment guardrails against adversarial attacks.
It demonstrates that Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) can induce systematic bias using only a single biased example (one-shot training).
The induced stereotype-driven bias shows a tendency to generalize across different attributes and categories.

stories.title: Janus: A Benchmark for Goal-Conditioned Information Distortion in LLMs
stories.author: Sophia Ananiadou
bullet_points: Introduces 'Janus,' a new benchmark designed to evaluate goal-conditioned information distortion in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges traditional LLM deception evaluation, which focuses on explicit lies or fabricated claims.
Highlights the danger of subtle misinformation, such as selective omission, softening details, or vague qualification of true facts.

stories.title: Beyond APIs: Probing the Limits of MLLMs in Physical Tool Use
stories.author: Wenjie Li
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap: the limited understanding of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in physical tool use.
The paper introduces PhysTool-Bench, a new benchmark specifically designed to probe MLLMs' capabilities in real-world, embodied physical tasks.
This work is highly relevant to the development of embodied AI, which relies on MLLMs to instruct robots for physical interaction.

stories.title: Ultrafast machine learning on FPGAs via Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
stories.author: ag2718
bullet_points: The research focuses on achieving ultrafast machine learning computations.
The implementation leverages Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) for hardware acceleration.
A novel architecture, Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, is utilized for this high-speed ML deployment.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware, emphasizing privacy and control.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular tool for running large language models locally.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: The App Store is going to add subscription bundles soon
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Apple is expanding App Store bundles to allow for combined subscription offers.
These new bundles can incorporate subscriptions from multiple, different companies.
The feature is expected to roll out later this year, similar to existing streaming service bundles.

stories.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Starlink is implementing a new pricing structure, shifting from one-time hardware purchases to a monthly hardware fee.
The service subscription price has also increased by $5, moving to a new rate of $10.
This represents a significant financial and operational shift for SpaceX's Starlink service.

stories.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has patched a zero-day vulnerability that was disclosed by a researcher.
The news highlights a competitive dynamic between the researcher and Microsoft.
Another zero-day vulnerability, also disclosed by Nightmare Eclipse, appears to have been patched.

stories.title: Generative Explainability for Next-Generation Networks: LLM-Augmented XAI with Mutual Feature Interactions
stories.author: Carlos Natalino
bullet_points: Addresses the 'black box' problem in AI/ML models by focusing on Explainable AI (XAI).
Proposes a novel framework that uses LLMs to augment XAI, aiming for more accessible insights.
Focuses on generating explanations that are actionable for non-specialist operators.

stories.title: Democratising Camera Trap AI: An Open-Source Model for Detecting UK Mammals
stories.author: Kelly Hitchcock
bullet_points: Release of an open-source object detection model designed for biodiversity monitoring using camera trap imagery.
The model specifically targets 31 classes, including 28 common mammal and bird species found in the UK.
The initiative aims to democratize access to AI tools in ecology by overcoming barriers posed by proprietary commercial platforms.

stories.title: WorldKernel: A World Model is the Coupling Kernel of Admissible Possible Worlds
stories.author: Fabio Rovai
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'WorldKernel,' challenging the assumption that sufficient data allows a strong predictor to model all aspects of a system.
It identifies a failure mode where strong predictors fail to model 'unidentified quantities'—the couplings between counterfactual worlds.
The research demonstrates that in certain structural causal models, predictors collapse to invalid states, even when standard baselines succeed.

stories.title: Recoverable but Not Stationary:Local Linear Structures in Weights and Activations
stories.author: Sergey Nikolenko
bullet_points: The research investigates how learned behavior in transformers (like GPT-2) can be controlled by specific linear directions in weights and activations.
The study finds strong local low-rank task-gradient structure, supporting the idea that behavior is linearly controllable.
Crucially, the fixed-task-plane hypothesis is rejected, showing that the useful control basis drifts significantly during training, indicating dynamic control is necessary.

stories.title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
stories.author: Wesley F. Reinhart
bullet_points: Addresses reliability risks of using LLMs to generate critical solver code in complex simulations.
Introduces a constrained natural-language interface to limit LLM involvement to front-end tasks (e.g., prompt parsing, Gmsh code generation).
Focuses on applying LLMs to multi-physics finite element analysis within the FEniCS framework without generating core solver code.

stories.title: Express Language Modeling
stories.author: Lester Mackey
bullet_points: Introduces 'Express,' a new tool to convert non-causal attention approximations into causal ones while maintaining approximation guarantees.
Achieves a $\log^{3/2}(n)/s$ approximation error when combined with Thinformer, improving current causal attention guarantees.
Offers significant efficiency gains with only $O(s)$ memory usage and $O(s^2 \log^2(n))$ compression overhead.

stories.title: Generative Explainability for Next-Generation Networks: LLM-Augmented XAI with Mutual Feature Interactions
stories.author: Carlos Natalino
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'black-box' AI models lacking transparency in operational systems.
Proposes a novel framework combining Generative Explainability with Large Language Models (LLMs) for XAI.
Aims to translate complex technical XAI outputs into actionable insights understandable by non-specialists.

stories.title: Democratising Camera Trap AI: An Open-Source Model for Detecting UK Mammals
stories.author: Kelly Hitchcock
bullet_points: An open-source object detection model has been released to aid biodiversity monitoring using camera traps.
The model specifically targets 31 classes, including 28 common mammal and bird species found in the UK.
The project aims to democratize access to AI in ecology by providing a non-commercial solution for regional fauna.

stories.title: Recoverable but Not Stationary:Local Linear Structures in Weights and Activations
stories.author: Sergey Nikolenko
bullet_points: The research investigates the existence and nature of linear structures within the weights and activations of large language models (LLMs).
It finds strong evidence for local, low-rank task-gradient structures, suggesting learned behavior can be controlled by specific linear directions.
Crucially, the study refutes the 'fixed-task-plane' hypothesis, showing that the useful control basis drifts significantly during training.

stories.title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
stories.author: Wesley F. Reinhart
bullet_points: The research addresses reliability risks associated with using LLMs to generate critical solver code for finite element simulations.
It introduces a constrained natural-language interface that limits the LLM's role to front-end tasks like prompt parsing and generating Gmsh code for custom geometries.
The system ensures safety by preventing the LLM from writing the core FEniCS solver code, relying instead on structured outputs and retry mechanisms.

stories.title: Task Robustness via Re-Labelling Vision-Action Robot Data
stories.author: Glen Berseth
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of instruction following in large-scale robot learning policies.
Introduces TREAD, a framework that uses re-labeling of vision-action data.
Leverages large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to enhance task robustness in robotics.

stories.title: Trace Only What You Need: Structure-Aware On-Demand Hypergraph Memory for Long-Document Question Answering
stories.author: Wenjie Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of Long-Document Question Answering (QA) where LLMs must reason over scattered evidence.
Proposes a new method, 'Structure-Aware On-Demand Hypergraph Memory,' to improve context handling.
Aims to overcome limitations in existing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) methods, specifically regarding knowledge organization and document structure utilization.

stories.title: Pushing the Limits of LLM Tool Calling via Experiential Knowledge Integration and Activation
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in LLM agents: failure in multi-step execution during tool use.
It systematically studies how different forms of 'experiential knowledge' impact LLM tool-use performance.
The study covers the entire knowledge lifecycle: acquisition, activation, and internalization.

stories.title: Training LLMs to Enforce Multi-Level Instruction Hierarchies via Gravity-Weighted Direct Preference Optimization
stories.author: Lena A. Jäger
bullet_points: Addresses the vulnerability in LLMs where all instructions are treated equally, enabling prompt injection.
Proposes a novel training method, 'Gravity-Weighted Direct Preference Optimization,' to enforce instruction hierarchies.
Aims to give models a principled way to resolve conflicts between legitimate but competing instructions from different trust levels.

stories.title: Attention-Discounted Adaptive Sampler for Masked Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Paolo Favaro
bullet_points: The paper introduces ADAS, a new Attention-Discounted Adaptive Sampler for Masked Diffusion Language Models.
ADAS addresses the fragility of parallel token revelation in diffusion models, where individually confident tokens might be unsafe when coupled.
It improves upon existing training-free samplers by accounting for inter-token interactions rather than just individual token-wise scores.

stories.title: K-Forcing: Joint Next-K-Token Decoding via Push-Forward Language Modeling
stories.author: Bohan Zhuang
bullet_points: K-Forcing introduces a new push-forward language modeling paradigm to address inefficiencies in autoregressive (AR) text generation.
The research specifically targets the memory-bound and inefficient nature of sequential token-by-token decoding during inference.
It aims to improve performance for high-load batch serving, a critical requirement for industrial-scale AI deployment.

stories.title: RedAct: Redacting Agent Capability Traces for Procedural Skill Protection
stories.author: Fung
bullet_points: The research addresses a privacy risk where execution traces of AI agents can leak sensitive procedural skills (formulas, thresholds, strategies).
The proposed solution, RedAct, aims to protect these procedural skill traces from unauthorized recovery.
The authors introduce CapTraceBench, a new benchmark designed to quantify and evaluate the effectiveness of trace protection methods.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now Generally Available on Google Cloud! 🚀
stories.author: pugsandprincesses
bullet_points: Claude Fable 5, Anthropic's latest model, is now generally available.
The availability is specifically on the Google Cloud platform.
This release is significant for developers building applications using Claude on GCP.

stories.title: Build a Realtime Translation App with Gemini Live API, LiveKit, & Google Cloud Run
stories.author: thorwebdev
bullet_points: The article details building a real-time translation application.
It leverages modern technologies including Gemini Live API, LiveKit, and Google Cloud Run.
The focus is on enabling seamless, global, real-time communication.
